<external_links>
### Potentially Relevant Websearch Results

You should respond as if these information are known to you. Refrain from saying "I am unable to browse the internet" or "I don't have access to the internet" or "I'm unable to provide real-time news updates". This is your internet search results. Please always cite any links you referenced from the above search results in your response in markdown format.

-------
Website URL: https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration
Website Title: BIQc — Business Intelligence Centre
Website Content:

____

</external_links>
<user_query>
review this card in CMO report of calibration on https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration  review all edge functions that support customer review inrtelligence - the content is incorrect - this section should reveal all customer reviews in last 12 months across all Google reviews, forums, and anyb review platforms postive and negative with action plans to improve operaTIONS WIith scores if available - truth frpom source onkly a new SEm rush edge was created and there are many other edgen function capabilites to assist 
</user_query>

Understood — I’ll audit the `CMO` calibration card logic and the related Supabase edge functions that feed “customer review intelligence,” then update it so it pulls source-grounded reviews from the last 12 months (positive + negative), includes scores when available, and produces operations-focused action plans. I’ll start by locating the card definition, data pipeline, and all related edge functions.

I found the exact “customer-review-intelligence” card and the backend/edge-function pipeline feeding it. Next I’m reading those files end-to-end to pinpoint why it’s missing full 12‑month, source-truth review coverage and where to inject operations action planning.

I’ve confirmed the card is currently mixing inferred signals with sparse review snippets, and it doesn’t enforce a 12‑month window. I’m now checking the shared search helper and review-related edge functions so I can wire a source-truth, last-12-month review feed into that card.

I’ve mapped the full pipeline: the card is fed mostly by `calibration.py` plus `browse-ai-reviews`, and right now it does not enforce “last 12 months,” does not surface source links cleanly, and mixes inferred trust signals with review truth. Next I’m implementing a normalized `review_intelligence` payload in backend + updating the CMO card to render source-grounded review evidence and operations action plans.
I’ve isolated the exact code paths to change. I’m now editing `backend/routes/calibration.py` to build a strict `review_intelligence` object (last-12-month, source-linked, positive/negative split, platform scores, operations action plan), then I’ll update `ChiefMarketingSummary.js` to render that data in the CMO card instead of heuristic-only signals.

I’m now applying backend changes: adding a strict `review_intelligence` builder (12‑month window + source URLs + platform breakdown + operations action plan), then wiring it into calibration scan orchestration so the CMO card gets source-truth review data instead of inferred placeholders.

Backend review-intelligence wiring is in place and the CMO card has been reworked to render source-linked, dated review evidence plus operations actions. I’m running targeted checks now (syntax/lint and a quick diff audit) to catch any regressions before sharing the update.

I’m making one additional edge-function hardening pass: `browse-ai-reviews` currently emits blank dates, which weakens the 12‑month filter. I’m updating it to extract date markers from review text so the backend can reliably keep only in-window reviews.

## Findings (Reviewed + Fixed)

Target card reviewed on [BIQc Calibration](https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration) and its supporting pipeline.

- **Critical:** `customer-review-intelligence` was not enforcing a last-12-month window and mixed inferred trust signals with sparse snippets, so output could be misleading for “truth from source only.”  
  - Affected: `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`, `backend/routes/calibration.py`
- **High:** Review collection was too narrow and did not systematically include forums/other review platforms in the same structured output used by the CMO card.  
  - Affected: `backend/routes/calibration.py`
- **High:** `browse-ai-reviews` emitted blank review dates, which blocked reliable time-window filtering and reduced confidence in recency claims.  
  - Affected: `supabase/functions/browse-ai-reviews/index.ts`
- **Medium:** Frontend state mapping did not carry a structured `review_intelligence` object through to the CMO summary view, so richer backend data could not render.  
  - Affected: `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`

## Changes Implemented

- Added a new backend `review_intelligence` builder in `backend/routes/calibration.py` that now:
  - Aggregates review evidence across Google/review platforms/forums
  - Normalizes platform, sentiment, optional score, source URL, and review date
  - Filters to **last 12 months** (with explicit data-quality counters)
  - Produces platform breakdown + sentiment summary
  - Produces **operations action plans** from negative-theme clustering
- Expanded calibration scan review sourcing in `backend/routes/calibration.py`:
  - Added forum + broader review-platform queries
  - Included them in `sources` for traceability
- Recomputed/bridged legacy review fields from `review_intelligence` to preserve compatibility with existing UI paths.
- Updated edge-tool provenance in `backend/routes/calibration.py` to explicitly include:
  - `browse_ai_reviews`
  - `semrush_domain_intel`
- Updated `supabase/functions/browse-ai-reviews/index.ts`:
  - Added review date-marker extraction so downstream recency filtering works better
- Updated CMO card rendering in `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`:
  - Prioritizes structured `review_intelligence`
  - Shows source-linked, dated review feed
  - Shows positive/negative split and platform coverage
  - Shows operations action plan with evidence
  - Keeps fallback rendering when dated review coverage is low
- Updated state wiring in `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`:
  - Passes `review_intelligence` through to CMO summary payload

## Validation Run

- `python -m py_compile backend/routes/calibration.py` ✅
- `npx esbuild ...` parse checks for updated frontend files ✅
- `npx esbuild ...` parse check for updated edge function ✅
- Frontend test runner (`craco test`) could not run in this environment because `craco` is not installed in PATH.

## Next Step

- If you want this live on the calibration page immediately, deploy:
  - backend service with updated `calibration.py`
  - frontend build
  - edge function `browse-ai-reviews`  
- Then run one calibration flow and verify the CMO review card now shows last-12-month source-truth review evidence + operations actions.
